pre_review_gate.reason: scoped test run did not complete: no JUnit XML produced by the scoped run (exit=1); stderr tail: /home/stijn/Documents/_code/SDD/fork/spec-kitty/.worktrees/drg-relation-parity-activation-gate-01KY48PD-lane-a/.venv/bin/python3: No module named pytest

reason:
Fixed the live stem<->canonical activation-gate bug (research.md D1). filter_graph_by_activation batch-resolves each per-kind activated_* set to canonical URNs ONCE per call (_resolve_activated_urns_by_kind) via charter.kind_vocabulary.resolve_artifact_urn; doctrine_root sourced from charter.catalog.resolve_doctrine_root() (never pack_roots[0]); unknown stems skip-with-report (UnknownArtifactIdError caught, never raised); anti_pattern excluded from stem resolution (no file-backed stem for that kind -- added PackContext.org_roots accessor to avoid a 3rd open-coded pack_roots[1:] copy. _node_is_activated now takes the pre-resolved map as an internal param and stays a pure full-URN membership check; PUBLIC gate signature unchanged.

Red-first evidence (T001, verified empirically before applying the fix): test_stem_form_activation_survives_the_gate was RED on merge-base (directive:DIRECTIVE_001 dropped because stem 001-architectural-integrity-standard never equals canonical DIRECTIVE_001); test_canonical_form_control_isolates_the_merge_base_defect (activated_directives={DIRECTIVE_001}) was GREEN on merge-base, isolating the cause as stem<->canonical mismatch not an incidental populated-list bug. Both surviving assertions differ post-fix: stem test is now GREEN; canonical-id test's assertion flips to 'excluded' because C-002 (require-canonical, contract row 4) makes a raw canonical id an unsupported activated_directives entry -- resolve_artifact_urn cannot resolve DIRECTIVE_001 as a stem (no file named that), so it is correctly skip-with-report post-fix. This is a documented, deliberate resolution of a conflict between the WP task doc's imprecise summary line and the authoritative contract; flagging for reviewer attention.

test_drg_filtering.py rationale (out-of-owned_files edit): this pre-existing file is NOT owned by any WP (checked WP02-WP05 owned_files -- none claim it), directly calls the private _node_is_activated with the old 3-arg signature, and 2 of its filter_graph_by_activation tests used synthetic non-real ids (dir-kept/dir-blocked) that never resolve post-fix. Left unfixed it would be permanently broken with no other agent responsible. Fixed as fallout: (1) _node_is_activated call sites updated to supply a hand-built resolved_urns_by_kind dict directly (keeps that test hermetic/synthetic, matching pre-WP01 intent); (2) the 2 filter_graph_by_activation tests switched to the real 001-architectural-integrity-standard/DIRECTIVE_001 stem<->canonical pair since the public function now does real resolution. All behavior-intent preserved; only the calling convention and id realism changed, consistent with research.md D1 ('stale assertion to update, not preserve').

Gates (all foreground): pytest tests/charter/test_drg_activation_gate.py tests/charter/test_drg_filtering.py -q -> 22 passed. pytest tests/charter/ -q -> 1651 passed, 1 skipped. pytest tests/charter/ tests/doctrine/ -q -> 4416 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed (up from 4402 passed/13 failed pre-fallout-fix; zero unexplained regressions). Diff-scoped lint: ruff check on drg.py/pack_context.py/test_drg_filtering.py/test_drg_activation_gate.py -> exit 0 all four. mypy --strict src/charter -> exit 1 but ONLY 7 pre-existing errors in write_pipeline.py/resynthesize_pipeline.py/mission_type_profiles.py (confirmed zero diff in those 3 files -- baseline red, not mine); mypy --strict on the 2 owned source files alone -> exit 0 clean. Commit fd7e82ada.
